# *epifitter*

[![CRAN](https://www.r-pkg.org/badges/version/epifitter)](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=epifitter)
[![Downloads](https://cranlogs.r-pkg.org/badges/grand-total/epifitter)](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=epifitter)

Provides a set of tools for aiding in the **visualization, description,
and comparison of plant disease progress curve** (DPC) data. A DPC
depict the change in a disease-intensity variable measured sequentially
at different times during the epidemics. Their analysis, that may
include fitting “classic” population dynamics models (e.g. logistic,
monomolecular, Gompertz), allows gaining understanding of the
epidemiological processes, but is most used for comparing epidemics.

The mathematics behind model fitting is straightforward and
general-purpose spreadsheet or statistical softwares can be used to
perform the calculations and select the “best” model. What `epifitter`
does is to provide the analyst with R functions for performing several
tasks commonly used for the temporal analysis of epidemics, including
graphical output.

Current implementation includes functions for the analyst to:

- Fit classic population dynamics models using linear and nonlinear
  approaches
- Select models based on statistical and visual analysis
- Calculate the area under the disease progress curve
- Compare epidemics via visual inference
- Simulate synthetic epidemics of various shapes and uncertainty

## Quick start

Install the stable release from CRAN.

``` r
install.packages("epifitter")
```

The development version of **epifitter** is available from GitHub. The
**devtools** package, available from CRAN, is required for installation.

``` r
if (!require(devtools)) {
  install.packages("devtools")
}

devtools::install_github("AlvesKS/epifitter")
```

## Meta

- Please [report any issues or
  bugs](https://github.com/AlvesKS/epifitter/issues).
- All code is licensed MIT
- To cite epifitter, please use the output from citation(“epifitter”)
- Please note that epifitter is released with [Contributor Code of
  Conduct](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.html). By
  participating in this project you agree to abide by its terms.

# Package index

## All functions

- [`AUDPC()`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/AUDPC.md) :
  Area under disease progress curve

- [`AUDPC_2_points()`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/AUDPC_2_points.md)
  : Estimate disease progress curve from only two points

- [`AUDPS()`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/AUDPS.md) :
  Area under disease progress stairs

- [`expo_fun()`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/expo_fun.md)
  : Function for Exponential model

- [`fit_lin()`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/fit_lin.md)
  : Fits epidemic models using data linearization

- [`fit_multi()`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/fit_multi.md)
  : Estimate model parameters for multiple disease progress curves

- [`fit_nlin()`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/fit_nlin.md)
  : Fits epidemic models using nonlinear aproach

- [`fit_nlin2()`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/fit_nlin2.md)
  : Fits epidemic models using nonlinear aproach. This function also
  estimates the maximum disease intensity parameter K

- [`gompi_fun()`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/gompi_fun.md)
  : Function for Gompertz model

- [`logi_fun()`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/logi_fun.md)
  : Function for logistic model

- [`mono_fun()`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/mono_fun.md)
  : Function for Monomolecular model

- [`plot_fit()`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/plot_fit.md)
  : Creates a plot panel for the fitted models

- [`PowderyMildew`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/PowderyMildew.md)
  : Dataset powdery mildew disease progress curves

- [`print(`*`<fit_lin>`*`)`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/print.fit_lin.md)
  :

  Print
  [`fit_lin()`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/fit_lin.md)
  or
  [`fit_nlin()`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/fit_nlin.md)
  outputs

- [`print(`*`<fit_nlin2>`*`)`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/print.fit_nlin2.md)
  :

  Print
  [`fit_nlin2()`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/fit_nlin2.md)
  outputs

- [`sim_exponential()`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/sim_exponential.md)
  : Simulate an epidemic using the Exponential model

- [`sim_gompertz()`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/sim_gompertz.md)
  : Simulate an epidemic using the Gompertz model

- [`sim_logistic()`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/sim_logistic.md)
  : Simulate an epidemic using the logistic model

- [`sim_monomolecular()`](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/reference/sim_monomolecular.md)
  : Simulate an epidemic using the Monomolecular model

- [Simulating disease progress
  curves](https://alvesks.github.io/epifitter/articles/simulation.md):
